summary: Comprehensive audit of the meeting pipeline system. Inspected 4 SQLite databases, 20+ Python scripts across v2 and v3 pipelines, the current Inbox filesystem state, 249 HITL queue items, and today's live-processed meeting manifest. Identified 7 breakpoints (gate bypass, calendar match always 0.0, no archive infrastructure, zombie v2 DB with 125 stuck meetings, write-only HITL queue, unpopulated meeting registry, dual DB confusion). Separated 10 reusable components from 5 broken/redesign-needed and 6 dead/deprecated modules. Recommended SQLite as derivative index rebuilt from filesystem manifests (Option A). Documented actual vs documented state machine with 5 discrepancies.
learnings: The v3 manifest.json files are the richest data source — richer than any SQLite DB. Redesign should treat them as primary.
calendar_match.py returns 0.0 confidence on every meeting — this is the single most impactful broken component since it cascades into HITL escalations.
Quality gate runs but its results are advisory-only — tick command catches gate exceptions as non-fatal. This is the critical interlock gap.
125 v2 meetings at queued_for_ai represent real Sep-Nov 2025 transcripts that were never block-processed. A recovery decision is needed.
The v3 pipeline works end-to-end (proven by today's 12-block generation) — the core is solid, the gaps are in gate enforcement, archive, and calendar integration.
